architecture_fit.note: Genuine softmax(Q·Kᵀ) with a hand-set 2-D Q/K projection — a small delta from base_model.py, single attention layer, no MLP, and sharpness ≈ 1.0 solves the segment-boundary task.

baseline_comparison.note: Uniform-attention strawman (sharpness 0) is measured under identical conditions; lift_over_linear_sharpness ≈ 0.999, plus an ablation reference line in the demo.

faithfulness.note: Segment-sign feature ablation (α→0) collapses sharpness to ~0, a real causal check, though somewhat self-evident for a hand-constructed circuit; README is honest about this.

operating_range.note: α swept over 4 orders of magnitude shows clear turn-on and saturation, but the axis is the internal feature strength rather than an input-scale axis (seg_len/spacing).

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-set torch QK projections on the GPU reproducing the target behaviour — exactly the hand-built-circuit bonus.

visual_judgement.note: Four purposeful panels (per-head sharpness bar, attention heatmap, α operating curve, ablation bar) make the claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: Why-this-visualisation section ties each chart to the benchmark's headline/lift, faithfulness, and scaling axis with justified y-axes and baselines.

notes: A clean hand-built boundary circuit expressed as real GPU dot-product attention, a minimal delta from base_model.py, achieving near-perfect boundary sharpness (≈0.999) against a uniform baseline of 0. Faithfulness ablation and a clear four-panel demo back the claim well; the main softness is that the operating-range sweep varies the internal feature strength rather than a realistic input axis. Mean of scored items ≈ 4.7 → good.
